# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions
on: [push]
jobs: 
    pipeline_job:   # job name that you can reference elsewhere in the YAML file using `${{ ... }}` syntax. 
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # specify execution environment (can be a specific version or pulls from cache if set) - here it will use latest available Ubuntu image with all necessary tools installed, which is our most reliable choice for GitHub actions runner in CI/CD pipeline workflow automation.   You can also choose to run on your local machine using `machine` strategy
        steps:  # list of operations a job has that are executed sequentially and can fail or succeed overall     - you should put all the necessary tasks here, which may include fetching dependencies (using tools like 'npm i' if it uses Node.js), building projects ('make build'), running tests etc.
            - name: Checkout code   # action to be performed in a new context  // check out your repo using git or hg    You can specify the pull request source here as well, which defaults back to master when not specified explicitly e.g `pull-requests` origin/main --strict    
                uses: actions/checkout@v2   # This action fetches repository contents and puts them into a GitHub Action runner environment variable (${{ secrets.[GITHUB_TOKEN] }})  // A token for the repo can be used, which is generated from settings or API. Defaults to `github` if not specified
             - name: Setup Node   # action on ubuntu only    You could have this run in another job with a timeout so it doesn't wait too long while dependencies are being installed (default 0)     If set, will install necessary version of node & npm. This step runs once before the 'run tests', which ensures all your software requirements up and running
                uses: actions/setup-node@v1    # setup Nodejs environment   - Actions has a special action for this too  (as can't set as we are not working in GitHub runner context, so it needs to be done on the machine where you have node.js installed)    
                with:            # Specify one or more references of actions/checkout#v2 that will run against the workflow container    // Passes along key-value pairs from steps and your job environment  (so `${{ runner.os }}`, `${{ runner.workspace }}/my_folder in $GITHUB_WORKSPACE is available here to be used in other jobs/steps -- can also reference the entire ecosystem config file
                  node-version: '14'   # You may set it based on your needs and then pass its value by using `${{ env.YOUR_ENV_VAR }}` syntax (Replace YOUR_ENV_VAR with what you defined in .env or equivalent)     Defaults to $nodeVersion which is set to the latest version available for all matures, if specified at job level they are overruled on environment/job-level defaults 
             - name: Install dependencies   # action that will be run before your tests (can use npm packages as inputs or in other steps)     With this step we install project's Node.js modules using `npm ci` which is faster than normal installing with yarn, pnpm etc., and gives us a clean environment
                run: |   # You can specify one command to be done before anything else (default 0 seconds after the last task)     This means if there are any tasks set up in this job step then it runs those first by default. If you want more granular control over when and how these steps happen, define them as separate jobs inside your workflow YAML
                  npm ci   # Here we run `npm install` to ensure our project dependencies will be installed correctly before anything else can occur in the job (uses 'package-lock' file or package.json)  // If you want other tools like yarn, pnpm etc., use their commands here and they would handle rest
             - name: Run tests   # Define steps for running your software under test from a set of scripts written as tasks inside this step     This task can be called by multiple jobs at different times within the same workflow. You could even run these in parallel or sequentially (default 'serial') based on what you want
                run: npm test   # Here we just call `npm test` to execute our tests using Node package manager for JavaScript project setup and dependencies installed beforehand, which can be useful if your software requires some specific version of any libraries/tools in its environment.  // Replace 'run' with command or script you want run inside the job (replace this line as per requirements)
             - name: Build   # Define steps for building project from scripts within a set named tasks     You can specify multiple commands here and they will be executed sequentially after previous step(s). Here we are using `npm build` to make sure our software is properly built before publishing it.  // Replace 'build' with command or script you want run inside the job (replace this line as per requirements)
                run: npm run build   # Run these steps in sequence after previous step(s), which means if there are any errors then only next set of tasks will be executed and fail, else they all get executed successfully.  // Replace 'run' with command or script you want to execute inside the job (replace this line as per requirements)
             - name: Publish   # Define steps for publishing package/folder into a repository from scripts within named task(s).     This step should be run after all tests have passed and before we make any final deployment decision.  // Replace 'publish' with command or script you want to execute inside the job (replace this line as per requirements)
                if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && branch=='main' }}   # Conditional step - only run after pushing in main/master(s), ie when code is being pushed, then continue otherwise not    // Only runs on the basis of push and master branches are set or if defined as such
                env:  # Define environment variable to be used within this task.     It can hold anything you like from strings (like namespaces/objects) in a safe way with ${{ ... }} syntax inside YAML, so it’s safer than trying harder for other tasks and steps at the same time or if they run concurrently
                  GITHUB_TOKEN: $${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}'   # Using `$$` to escape backticks because we want YAML markup here in text, not as a literal string  // You can use this token for deploying on production when code is pushed directly from GitHub actions workflow
                run: |    # Action that will be performed inside the job.     If you specify multiple commands then they get executed sequentially and must succeed to trigger further tasks in YAML after each command (default 0 seconds). You can set timeout duration, which defaults back with .NET Core/.Net appsettings here if not specified
                  echo "🔨 Building production ready static files..."   # Here we are just doing a comment for the sake of indicating what is happening inside our job. Replace 'echo' and commands as per requirements using ${{ ... }} or other YAML markup syntax to add details about jobs/steps being run
                  npm publish --access public  // Publish package(s) /folder into a repository in the NPM registry (--acccess can be either "public"或为私有). Default is 'public' if not specified. In case of multiple packages, they are automatically made private and their access mode to make them publicly available will depend on .env file or equivalent
